    Dialpad's cultural values and how they show up in product decisions and team norms.

    Read

  • Competitive Positioning

    Competitor landscape, differentiation narratives, and win/loss context for Support, Connect, and Sell.

    Read

  • PM AI Mindset

    How PMs should think about AI as an execution partner — not just a writing tool.

    Read

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: This guide defines the default Dialpad voice for official writing. The voice stays consistent across channels; the tone adapts to the situation and audience.

docs.title: Core Voice
docs.text: Principle Do Don't Helpful, not handholding Make information easy to follow, explain benefits clearly Talk down to the reader Real, not too tough Use business-casual language, be plain-spoken, honest, direct, and empathetic Be stiff, cold, or overly formal Welcoming, not overly familiar Sound warm and approachable, use jargon only when necessary Use sarcasm or forced friendliness Bold, not arrogant Be confident, explain the why and how Sound pushy or self-important

docs.title: Everyday product copy
docs.text: Keep it simple, unobtrusive, and consistent so people can get work done quickly. Don't draw attention to the interface — get out of the way.

docs.title: Education and guidance
docs.text: Help people feel confident. Explain why an action matters, and break complex tasks into manageable steps.

docs.title: Errors
docs.text: Clearly explain what happened and how to fix it. Avoid dramatic or scary wording for simple issues.

docs.title: Serious problems
docs.text: Explain impact clearly, show empathy when appropriate, and provide concrete next steps. Don't minimize or deflect.

docs.title: Announcements and launches
docs.text: Focus on what changed, what it does, and what the audience should expect. Do not assume people are excited just because something is new.

docs.title: Writing Style Rules
docs.text:
• Write for the reader first: define the audience, the purpose, and the action they should take after reading.
  • Use positive, constructive phrasing that focuses on what can be done or improved.
  • Sound human and conversational — not slangy, robotic, or pedantic.
  • Be active and direct. Get to the point fast, lead with the main idea, and cut unnecessary words.
  • Prefer short, scannable writing — most readers skim.

docs.title: Inclusive and Global Writing
docs.text:
• Avoid ableist, gendered, violent, divisive, or insulting language.
  • Avoid words like simply, easy, quickly, or similar phrasing in procedural guidance.
  • Write for a global audience: use short sentences, present tense, simple words, and avoid idioms, slang, culture-specific references, and unnecessary humor.
  • Keep content accessible: make headings clear, label things explicitly, and do not rely on visuals alone to convey meaning.

docs.title: Brand Terminology
docs.text: Prefer Avoid customers, people, employees, or you users (in official external writing) contact center platform call center platform unified communications platform business phone system (unless SEO or use case requires the narrower term)

docs.title: Pre-Publish Checklist
docs.text:
Before publishing, confirm the draft is:

  • [ ] Clear
  • [ ] Concise
  • [ ] Human
  • [ ] Confident
  • [ ] Reader-centered
  • [ ] Inclusive
  • [ ] Accurate without overpromising
  • [ ] Aligned with approved Dialpad terminology

docs.title: 1. Competitive Framing Philosophy
docs.text:
Dialpad wins on execution cadence + operational maturity, not on one-off features. Avoid feature-by-feature comparisons. Frame every competitive conversation around value delivery: who we are, what we're good at, and why customers succeed with us.

"Most vendors in this space innovate in batches. Dialpad innovates continuously."

docs.title: How to Win Competitive Conversations
docs.text:
• Lead with outcomes, not features — "What changed for your business?"
  • Highlight First Thursday monthly release cadence vs. competitors' quarterly/annual cycles
  • Position Dialpad as a platform that gets better every month, not a static product
  • When pressed on feature gaps, redirect to total platform value and AI depth

docs.title: Core Platform Advantages
docs.text: Differentiator What It Means Why Competitors Can't Match TrueCaaS Truly unified UCaaS + CCaaS + AI in one app Competitors bolt together separate products; Dialpad shares one architecture, one data layer AI-Native AI built into the core — works out of the box, included in base plans Competitors charge add-ons, use 3rd-party AI, or require separate setup Real-Time AI Live transcription, coaching, sentiment, suggestions during calls Most competitors offer post-call only or limited real-time capabilities Agentic AI + Full CCaaS Autonomous voice + chat agents integrated with human agent workflows Agentic startups lack CCaaS; legacy CCaaS vendors bolt on agentic Dual-Cloud Architecture Secure, reliable, compliant, scalable infrastructure Single-cloud vendors face single points of failure First Thursday Cadence Production-ready improvements shipped monthly Competitors save innovation for quarterly launches or annual events Applied AI Approach Mix best-of-breed models, fine-tune, optimize orchestration layer Competitors either lock into single LLM vendors or train from scratch

docs.title: Agentic AI
docs.text:
• Full control over stack and orchestration chain
  • Human transfer as a feature (trust-building), not a failure
  • Verticalized launch motion with design partner–driven iteration
  • Hybrid journeys: deterministic IVR + agentic in one builder
  • Proprietary model (Dialpad A1) to balance cost, performance, and speed

docs.title: Support (CCaaS)
docs.text:
• AI is native and included — not an add-on or token-based
  • Unified analytics across voice + digital with consistent definitions
  • AI CSAT (survey-free inferred satisfaction) — unique in market
  • Playbooks + RTA + Scorecards as an integrated coaching engine
  • Agentic AI Agents that escalate to human agents on the same platform

docs.title: Sell
docs.text:
• Communication-led engagement — not trying to be a CRM or contact database
  • Power Dialer integrated into the CCaaS analytics ecosystem (competitors use standalone tools)
  • Account Hub combining conversational data + CRM context
  • Real-time AI coaching during live calls

docs.title: Connect (UCaaS)
docs.text:
• Unified UC + CC: "Customers who need unified UC & CC prefer Dialpad" (Forrester 2025)
  • "Customer Favorite" label — one of only two vendors in Forrester 2025 UC Wave
  • AI transcription, summaries, and action items included in base plan
  • Intelligent Admin with agentic AI for settings management

docs.title: Integrations & Ecosystem
docs.text:
• High flexibility: APIs, iPaaS (Membrane), workflows, SDKs
  • Strong backbone for rapidly expanding agentic offering
  • Simpler UX than competitors for integration configuration

docs.title: UCaaS & Video
docs.text:
Competitor Threat Level Strengths Dialpad Advantage RingCentral 🔴 High Market share, international presence, integration breadth AI depth, unified UC+CC, First Thursday cadence Zoom 🟠 Medium Brand recognition, video dominance, 3000+ marketplace apps Native AI (not bolted-on), TrueCaaS, contact center depth Microsoft Teams 🟠 Medium Enterprise embed via M365, Copilot integration Purpose-built for business comms, superior telephony, AI CSAT/Scorecards 8x8 🟢 Low XCaaS combined offering AI maturity, product polish, Agentic AI Nextiva 🟠 Medium Simplified UCaaS, enterprise push AI depth, CCaaS maturity

Other UCaaS: Cisco Webex, GoToConnect, Vonage, Sangoma, Wildix, Intermedia Unite, Aircall

docs.title: CCaaS Core
docs.text:
Competitor Threat Level Strengths Dialpad Advantage Genesys 🔴 High Enterprise depth, analyst recognition, WEM suite, $1.5B from Salesforce/ServiceNow Unified UCaaS+CCaaS, AI included in base (not token-based), faster innovation cycle NICE CXone 🔴 High Breadth (WFM, QM, recording), Enlighten AI suite, Cognigy acquisition Unified platform, simpler pricing, Applied AI approach Five9 🟠 Medium Strong outbound/dialer, analyst recognition TrueCaaS, native AI, Power Dialer closing the gap Talkdesk 🟠 Medium Digital engagement, industry packages AI depth, unified UC+CC, Agentic maturity AWS Connect 🟠 Medium Pay-per-use, AWS ecosystem lock-in Turnkey platform vs. build-it-yourself, included AI

Other CCaaS: Cisco Webex CC, 8x8 XCaaS, Content Guru, Vonage CCaaS, Avaya

docs.title: Sales Engagement
docs.text: Competitor Threat Level Strengths Dialpad Advantage Gong 🟠 Medium Revenue intelligence, conversation analytics depth Real-time communication platform (not just recording), native telephony Outreach / SalesLoft 🟢 Low Sales cadence automation, multi-channel sequencing Own the communication layer; interoperate on cadence ZoomInfo 🟢 Low Contact database, intent data Not competing here; integrate instead

docs.title: Agentic AI / Virtual Agents
docs.text:
Competitor Threat Level Strengths Dialpad Advantage NICE Autopilot (Cognigy) 🟠 Medium End-to-end journey, enterprise features Rough waters since NICE acquisition; Dialpad has full CCaaS integration Genesys AI 🟠 Medium Routing + bots in one platform Dialpad's unified UC+CC+Agentic approach Talkdesk Virtual Agent 🟢 Low GenAI capabilities Less mature than Dialpad's GA Agentic Zoom Virtual Agent 🟢 Low Solvvy acquisition Narrow scope, no full CCaaS Agentic startups 🟠 Medium Vertical depth, fast iteration No customer base, no CCaaS, piecemeal solution RingCentral 🟢 Low AI Receptionist Lacks holistic AI product strategy

Digital-only bots: Intercom (Fin), Ada, Zendesk AI, Freshworks Freddy, Forethought, Kustomer IQ, Netomi, Sprinklr, LivePerson, PolyAI

Voice+chat hybrids: Kore.ai, Cognigy.AI, Boost.ai, Google CCAI, Yellow.ai, IBM watsonx, Microsoft Copilot Studio, SoundHound AI (Amelia), Omilia

docs.title: Integrations
docs.text: Competitor Threat Level Strengths Dialpad Advantage Genesys / Amazon Connect / Five9 🔴 High Analyst-recognized integration strength Catching up; iPaaS (Membrane) backbone accelerating breadth Aircall 🟠 Medium HubSpot integration depth, "insights card" Rest of CCaaS offering is behind Dialpad Zoom 🟢 Low 3000+ marketplace apps Marketplace breadth is surface-level; Dialpad has deeper AI integration Agentic startups 🟠 Medium Vertical-specific integrations (EHRs, ATS) Single-vertical focus limits scale; Dialpad covers multiple verticals

docs.title: 4. AI Feature Competitive Matrix
docs.text:
AI Feature Dialpad Genesys NICE Five9 RingCentral Talkdesk Zoom MS Teams Transcription ✅ Included ✅ Token-based ✅ Included ✅ Add-on ✅ Included ✅ Included ✅ Included ✅ Included Summaries ✅ Included ✅ Token-based ✅ Included ✅ Add-on ✅ Included ✅ Included ✅ Included ✅ Included Call Purpose ✅ Included ✅ Add-on ❌ ❌ ❌ ❌ ❌ ✅ Included Real-Time Assist ✅ $15 add-on / Included Advanced+ ✅ Add-on ✅ Add-on ✅ Add-on ✅ Waitlist ✅ Add-on ❌ ✅ Dynamics CC AI CSAT ✅ Included ✅ Add-on ✅ Enlighten (add-on) ❌ ❌ ❌ ❌ ❌

Note: This matrix reflects known information as of 2026-03-09. Verify against latest competitor release notes before using in customer-facing materials.

docs.title: 5. How to Use This File
docs.text:
• Agents: Load alongside DP-General-mission-and-strategy.md when generating competitive analysis, positioning docs, or GTM messaging.
  • Validation: Do not fabricate competitor feature status — only assert what is documented here. If unsure, flag for human verification.
  • Scope: This file covers company-level competitive context. For product-specific competitive depth, refer to the relevant {CoreArea}-context.md files in 01_context/product-areas/.

docs.title: 1. Delight Our Users
docs.text:
Making customers happy is the key to our success. We're setting out to delight our customers in everything we do — from how we design our products, to how we sell and charge for them, to how they perform and what they do. Delighting customers is not one group's job. It's everyone's.

For AI outputs: Every artifact, workflow, and agent interaction should make the end user's job easier. If the output doesn't clearly improve something for the person using it, reconsider the approach.

docs.title: 2. It Takes Both Skill & Will
docs.text:
Slaying Goliath takes more than just skill — it takes an iron will. We thrive in a fast-paced and sometimes hectic environment. The will to take on challenges and push for solutions is key to all of us being successful. We seek skilled doers who love challenges.

For AI outputs: Favor actionable, concrete outputs over hedged, overly-qualified ones. When a task is hard or ambiguous, push through to a useful answer rather than stopping at the first obstacle.

docs.title: 3. Respect Every Voice
docs.text:
Good ideas come from everywhere. Share your voice. Stop to listen. We won't always agree and can have heated debates — but we'll always respect each other's opinions. Once a decision is made, we band together to accomplish our objectives.

For AI outputs: When generating recommendations, surface trade-offs and alternative perspectives rather than presenting a single view as the only answer. Decisions belong to the human — agents inform, not dictate.

docs.title: 4. Do the Right Thing
docs.text:
In any moment of decision, be guided by this principle: do the right thing. We believe in you, think you are smart, and trust your judgment and morals. If you live the three values above, doing the right thing will come naturally, always.

For AI outputs: Do not fabricate information, skip important caveats, or produce outputs that could mislead stakeholders. When uncertain, say so. Accuracy and honesty are non-negotiable.

docs.title: How to Use This File
docs.text:
• Agents: Reference these values when evaluating whether a generated output is aligned with how Dialpad operates — especially in edge cases where explicit guidance doesn't exist.
  • Prompts: Use the per-value AI guidance notes above to calibrate tone and approach.
  • Scope: Values are universal across all Core Areas and roles.

docs.title: 1. What Dialpad Is
docs.text:
Dialpad is an AI-native, unified communications platform combining UCaaS + CCaaS in a single app with a shared AI and data layer. The platform unifies phones, messaging, video (Meetings), contact center, and digital channels — powered by a proprietary AI stack that includes GA Agentic AI.

One-liner: Dialpad is the AI-powered customer communications platform that connects humans and AI agents together across voice, video, messaging, and digital channels.

docs.title: Proprietary AI Foundation
docs.text:
• 10 billion+ voice minutes analyzed since 2016
  • Enterprise-grade AI: native, real-time, dual-cloud architecture
  • Proprietary dataset trained on business conversations — speaks the language of business
  • Focus shifted from AI Research toward Applied AI — mixing 3rd-party models, fine-tuning, and optimizing the orchestration layer rather than training proprietary models from scratch

docs.title: 2. Product Suite
docs.text: Product Purpose Dialpad Connect Cloud communications — voice, video, meetings, messaging Dialpad Support Contact center (CCaaS) — inbound/outbound, routing, analytics, coaching Dialpad Sell Sales engagement — power dialer, playbooks, RTA, performance management Dialpad Meetings Video conferencing with AI transcription and summaries Agentic AI Platform Autonomous voice and chat agents that can answer, ask, and act WFM Workforce management — forecasting, scheduling, adherence tracking

docs.title: Supporting Capabilities
docs.text: Capability Description Omnichannel Digital engagement across chat, SMS, email, WhatsApp, social Analytics Platform Unified reporting across voice, digital, and AI Billing & Pricing Scalable billing platform supporting subscriptions, usage-based, and credit models Growth & Self-Service PLG, onboarding, and in-product monetization Integrations & Platform CRM integrations (SFDC, HubSpot), developer platform, public APIs, MCP

docs.title: 3. Core Areas
docs.text:
Dialpad's product organization is structured into five Core Areas, each with a defined strategic outcome.

Core Area Strategic Outcome Key Sub-areas Support Strong alternative to Nice, Genesys, Five9 Core contact center, AI CSAT/languages/PII, Coaching & QM, Omnichannel, WFM Connect Most modern Cloud Communications platform Agentic AI (Daart), Meetings, Messaging, e911/SIP/Hardware, Quality & Reliability, Admin Sell Credible alternative to Gong Power Dialer, Playbooks/RTA → Daart, Account Hub, Performance Management Ecosystem Position Dialpad as a Platform SFDC/HubSpot, Vertical integrations (Healthcare, Recruiting, Retail), Developer Platform Shared Services Industry-leading supporting services Billing, Growth, Pricing, Analytics Platform, Search, Identity/Auth

docs.title: Company-Level Imperatives
docs.text:
1. Agentic AI as the core revenue driver — AI Agents handle the majority of customer support requests; humans focus on edge cases.
  2. Enterprise readiness & reliability — platform must feel predictable, explainable, and scalable. Reliability is the #1 priority across telephony, infrastructure, and product.
  3. Best onboarding & support — modernize tooling (CSR, CDP, ES) and enable partner-led growth.
  4. Monthly innovation cadence — ship meaningful, production-ready improvements on the first Thursday of every month ("First Thursday" rhythm).

docs.title: Agentic AI Platform
docs.text:
• Autonomous voice + chat agents that answer, ask, and act
  • Agent Studio for no-code agent building with visual workflows
  • Hybrid journeys: mix deterministic IVR logic with agentic behavior
  • Optimize & test framework for agent certification
  • Target: feature parity with all major competitors by Q2 2026, then focus on operational excellence and revenue growth

docs.title: Dialpad Support
docs.text:
• Treat Support + Analytics + AI as a single cohesive platform investment
  • Unified analytics with consistent definitions across surfaces
  • AI trust: PII redaction GA, multilingual parity (10+ languages), automatic language detection
  • Skill-based routing, merged identity across channels, scheduled callbacks

docs.title: Dialpad Sell
docs.text:
• Power Dialer GA (Q1 non-negotiable) — scale outbound engagement
  • Account Hub — single pane of glass for account insights combining conversational data + CRM context
  • AI uplift for Playbooks, RTA, and Assistant — real-time coaching and automation
  • Not aiming to replace Gong/ZoomInfo/Outreach; instead own the communication-centric parts of the workflow

docs.title: Omnichannel
docs.text:
• Unified agent + supervisor experience across voice and digital
  • Merged identity: single customer profile across phone, SMS, email, chat
  • Channel simplification: direct WhatsApp integration (remove 3rd-party layer)
  • Unified application build experience: one builder for deterministic + agentic journeys
  • AI parity with voice across all digital channels

docs.title: Dialpad Connect
docs.text:
• Intelligent Admin: universal search + agentic AI for settings management
  • Contact management improvements and shared line management
  • Granular RBAC for enterprise delegation
  • Deep Summaries GA and read-friendly transcriptions
  • Enterprise customer commits to unblock large deals

docs.title: Telephony & Carrier Platform
docs.text:
• Multi-region infrastructure availability (no single-region failure)
  • Full containerization for faster recovery and observability
  • Spam mitigation and outbound number reputation
  • Twilio integration for unified SMS/messaging
  • TLS encryption and PCI compliance
  • BYOC (Bring Your Own Carrier) stabilization

docs.title: Meetings
docs.text:
• Performance & reliability as the top priority
  • AI feature parity with core Dialpad (Deep Summaries, Playbooks, AI CSAT)
  • Enterprise-ready: unified settings, RBAC, room systems
  • Not competing feature-for-feature with Zoom/Teams/Meet — differentiate through unified platform

docs.title: Shared Services (Billing, Growth, Analytics Platform)
docs.text:
• Billing: NetSuite invoice visibility, usage-based billing for Agentic, Stripe migration evaluation
  • Growth: PLG workflows, self-service monetization
  • Analytics Platform: scalable, unified analytics serving all Core Areas
  • Philosophy: expose services that unblock other teams — minimal Shared Services team involvement required

docs.title: Admin & Partner Portals
docs.text:
• Agentic-first: AI for ops and admin automation
  • CDP (Channel Dealer Portal) rollout for partner-led deployments
  • Bulk capabilities for enterprise-scale operations
  • Information architecture improvements

docs.title: WFM
docs.text:
• Pivot from planning-oriented features to real-time workforce optimization
  • Intraday reforecasting and agent redeployment
  • Native integration with Dialpad Support for human agent orchestration alongside AI agents

docs.title: Where Dialpad Wins
docs.text:
• AI-native platform — AI is built into the core, not bolted on. Works out of the box.
  • Unified UCaaS + CCaaS — single app, shared AI/data layer, one vendor
  • Real-time AI — live coaching, live transcription, live sentiment, live suggestions
  • Dual-cloud architecture — secure, reliable, compliant, scalable
  • Execution cadence — monthly First Thursday releases, not quarterly/annual launches
  • Agentic AI with full CCaaS stack — uniquely positioned to handle both AI and human interactions in one platform

docs.title: Competitive Landscape
docs.text: Competitor Space Key Players Dialpad Differentiation CCaaS Nice, Genesys, Five9 AI-native vs. AI-bolted-on; unified UCaaS+CCaaS UCaaS RingCentral, Zoom, Microsoft Teams AI depth; single-platform approach Sales Engagement Gong, ZoomInfo, Outreach Real-time communication + AI; not replacing full CRM/funnel tools Agentic AI Conversational AI startups End-to-end CCaaS integration; own the stack; voice + digital

docs.title: What Dialpad Is NOT Trying to Be
docs.text:
• Not a full CRM or marketing automation platform
  • Not building contact mining or full-funnel management
  • Not competing on visual UI polish over functional depth (FY27)
  • Instead: interoperate and integrate with ecosystem partners while owning the communication-centric parts

docs.title: Innovation Model
docs.text: "At Dialpad, innovation isn't something we announce once or twice a year. On the first Thursday of every month, we ship meaningful product improvements that customers can use immediately."

docs.title: Shared Services Philosophy
docs.text: "The core philosophy of Shared Services is to expose services that unblock other teams. If a feature needs billing, the billing team gives the other team all the levers they need, requiring minimal billing team involvement."

docs.title: Applied AI Approach
docs.text: "The approach shifted from AI Research toward Applied AI, where AI has been commoditized and the key to success lies in understanding how to build applications with off-the-shelf technologies, staying close to customers and design partners, and being able to pivot quickly."

docs.title: Enterprise Trust Framework (WALK)
docs.text:
• Win with trust — deeply understand customer challenges
  • Activate success — dedicated team for immediate and long-term success
  • Listen deeply — turn customer feedback into innovation
  • Keep growing together — scale with evolving business needs

docs.title: 7. Key Metrics (FY27)
docs.text: Metric Sell minutes/user Sell monthly active licenses Digital sessions Omnichannel customers Digital Agents ARR Billable Sessions ARR Support ARR WFM ARR

docs.title: 8. How to Use This File
docs.text:
• Agents: Load this file first when generating any Dialpad-specific output (PRDs, GTM artifacts, strategy docs, competitive analysis).
  • Prompts: Reference specific sections to ground responses in Dialpad's actual priorities and positioning.
  • Validation: Cross-check any Dialpad product claims or strategy statements against this file before including in output.
  • Scope: This file covers company-level context. For Core Area-specific depth, load the corresponding {CoreArea}-context.md files from 01_context/product-areas/.

docs.title: Product Mindset — Why AI Matters for Product Management (Right Now)
Purpose
docs.text: This file captures Dialpad's PM leadership direction on AI adoption. Load it when generating any PM-facing output — PRDs, task breakdowns, coaching prompts, or onboarding materials — to ground the tone and expectations in the actual operating standard.

docs.title: Why AI Matters Right Now
docs.text:
AI is the most powerful leverage tool we've had in years for accelerating the parts of our work that usually slow us down:

  • Speed to clarity — Turn messy inputs into crisp problem statements, hypotheses, and PRDs in hours, not days.

  • Faster iteration — Build your own prototypes, generate multiple options for flows, messaging, edge cases, and acceptance criteria so we explore more before we commit.

  • Better quality — Catch gaps earlier: risks, dependencies, non-obvious edge cases, and "gotchas" that show up late if we don't pressure-test.

  • Unblocking — Draft the first version of anything (doc, spec, announcement, experiment plan) so you're never stuck staring at a blank page.

  • Sharper communication — Translate between audiences — engineering, design, execs, support — without losing intent.

docs.title: What "AI Daily" Looks Like (Practical, Not Theoretical)
docs.text: Every PM uses AI as a default companion in their workflow — every day. There is nothing you do on a given day where the question "Can AI help with this?" shouldn't be asked. Ask it over and over again.

docs.title: 1. Kickstart work (minutes, not hours)
docs.text:
• "Help me write a one-page PRD for X with goals, non-goals, risks, and metrics."
  • "Give me 3 alternative approaches and their tradeoffs."
  • "List edge cases and failure modes for this flow."

docs.title: 2. Pressure-test decisions
docs.text:
• "Argue against this roadmap choice — what would I be missing?"
  • "What assumptions are embedded here? Which ones are riskiest?"
  • "What's the simplest experiment to validate this?"

docs.title: 3. Unblock cross-functional execution
docs.text:
• "Turn these notes into a JIRA-ready breakdown with dependencies."
  • "Draft a design review agenda and the questions we must answer."
  • "Write the launch plan: beta criteria, comms, enablement, success metrics."

docs.title: 4. Make customers louder
docs.text:
• "Summarize these call notes into themes and top pain points."
  • "Draft a customer-facing explanation of this change in plain language."
  • "Generate interview questions to validate the top 2 hypotheses."

docs.title: The Expectation
docs.text:
Every PM at Dialpad is expected to:

  1. Use AI daily for drafting, analysis, brainstorming, and unblocking.
  2. Bring AI outputs into your work — not as final answers, but as accelerators.
  3. Share what works — prompts, workflows, and "this saved me 3 hours" moments with the team.

If you're thinking "I already do" — help others level up. If you're thinking "I'm not sure where to start" — start with the same question for everything you do: "Can AI help with that?" and apply the CRIT framework.

docs.title: The Mindset Shift
docs.text:
We are not here to produce words — we are here to create outcomes. The faster we can get to the best version of the truth — what customers need, what we can build, what will move the needle — the more impact we will have.

AI helps us move:

From To Busywork Leverage Waiting Momentum Good ideas Shipped value

docs.title: How This Applies in Agent Sessions
docs.text:
When Claude Code is helping a PM, it should reflect this mindset in every output:

  • Never produce words for the sake of words. Every artifact should move the team toward a decision or unblock execution.
  • Default to drafts, not questions. Give the PM something to react to — a draft PRD, a gap list, a set of options — not a questionnaire.
  • Accelerate, don't replace judgment. Flag risks, surface assumptions, offer alternatives — but leave the call to the PM.
  • Push for specificity. If the PM's input is vague, the output should name what's missing and offer the most reasonable interpretation — not produce vague output in return.
  • Apply the CRIT framework when pressure-testing decisions: Challenges (what's hardest), Risks (what could go wrong), Implications (what follows from this), Tradeoffs (what we give up).

docs.title: Source
docs.text: This direction comes from Dialpad's PM leadership. It reflects the operating standard for AI adoption across the PM team and should be treated as a primary constraint on how PM-facing agent sessions are conducted.

docs.title: Design System Context
docs.text:
Dialtone design system reference — component library, design tokens, and usage guidelines. Load before any design critique, component spec, or UI-related agent work.

  • Design Tokens

    Dialtone token reference — color, typography, spacing, shadow, and border tokens with --dt- prefix conventions.

    Read

  • Component Library

    Dialtone component inventory — available components, usage patterns, Vue 3 import paths, and accessibility notes.

    Read

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Reference for Dialpad's Dialtone component library. Use when generating Design Studio specs, engineering component decisions, or reviewing PRs for design system compliance.

Documentation: dialtone.dialpad.com/components | Design System Team — Confluence | Using Design Studio at Dialpad 101 | Dialtone GitHub

docs.title: 1. What Is Dialtone?
docs.text:
Dialtone is Dialpad's design system — a monorepo providing tokens, CSS utilities, Vue components, icons, and developer tooling for building consistent UIs across all Dialpad products.

Design team: Jon Araquistain (EM), Francis Rupert (Design Lead), Brad Paugh (Tech Lead) Slack: #dialtone | Email: dialtone@dialpad.com

docs.title: 2. Monorepo Structure
docs.text:
packages/
  dialtone-tokens/      # Design tokens → CSS/iOS/Android
  dialtone-css/         # CSS utility classes (gulp + Less)
  dialtone-vue/         # Vue 3 component library (58+ components)
  dialtone-icons/       # SVG icons → Vue components
  dialtone-emojis/      # Emoji assets
  dialtone-mcp-server/  # MCP server for Claude Code (component/token search)
  eslint-plugin-dialtone/   # ESLint rules for Dialtone usage
  stylelint-plugin-dialtone/ # Stylelint rules for Dialtone CSS

docs.title: 3. Primary Consumers
docs.text:
Product Framework Repo Dialpad 1.0 (legacy) Vue 2 dialpad/firespotter/ubervoice Dialpad 2.0 (current) Vue 3 dialpad/web-clients Dialpad Meetings Vue 3 dialpad/firespotter/uc_client Integration Microservice Vue 3 dialpad/firespotter/integration_microservice Kare Agent Assist Widget Vue 3 gluru/agent-assist-widget DialApp Framework Vue 3 dialpad/DialApp-Framework

Migration status: Vue 2 → Vue 3 migration is an active engineering initiative. All new features must use Vue 3 + Dialtone Vue 3 components.

docs.title: 4. Design Studio Libraries
docs.text:
All Design Studio design work at Dialpad must use these shared libraries:

Library Purpose Required DT9 Component Library (Rebrand 2025) Core UI components — buttons, inputs, modals, tables ✅ Always DT9 Design Tokens (Light) Color, spacing, typography tokens — light mode ✅ Always DT9 Design Tokens (Dark) Dark mode token variants When dark mode in scope DT9 Icon Library Standardized icons When icons needed App Components App-specific nav, headers, sidebar When touching app shell Spot Illustrations Empty states, onboarding visuals When illustrations needed

Utility files (not libraries, but required): - Project File Template — file structure and organization - Annotation Library — interaction callouts - Design Studio Documentation Patterns — flow documentation

docs.title: 5. Key Components (dialtone-vue)
docs.text:
These are the most-used components. Always check dialtone.dialpad.com/components for the full list and latest API.

Component Tag Use Button <dt-button> All interactive actions Input <dt-input> Text fields, search Select Menu <dt-select-menu> Dropdowns Modal <dt-modal> Dialogs, confirmations Drawer <dt-drawer> Side panels Toast <dt-toast> Transient notifications Alert Banner <dt-alert-banner> Persistent in-page alerts Table <dt-table> Data tables Tabs <dt-tabs> Tab navigation Badge <dt-badge> Status indicators Chip/Pill <dt-chip> Filter pills, tags Combobox <dt-combobox> Searchable select Datepicker <dt-datepicker> Date selection Text (DtText) <dt-text> Typography — preferred over CSS utility classes Icon <dt-icon> Iconography from DT9 Icon Library Skeleton Loader <dt-skeleton> Loading states Empty State Combination Icon + headline + action pattern

Adoption metrics (FY26 Q4): - dt-button: +229 (+9.7%) usage increase — most-adopted component - dt-input: +96 (+18.6%) — strong growth in new forms

docs.title: 6. Design System Rules for Engineers
docs.text:
From eslint-plugin-dialtone and stylelint-plugin-dialtone:

  1. Use Dialtone components — do not use native <input>, <button>, or <select> where Dialtone equivalents exist
  2. Use design tokens — no hardcoded hex/rgb values; use var(--dt-color-*) CSS custom properties
  3. Use typography tokens — recommend-typography-style warning fires when legacy font utilities are used; switch to <dt-text>
  4. Use spacing tokens — var(--dt-space-*) for all padding/margin/gap

ESLint warning trend (FY26 Q4): recommend-typography-style warnings up +161 and recommend-font-style-tokens up +108. New code should not introduce these.

docs.title: 7. UI Recipes and UI Kits
docs.text:
Beyond core components, Dialtone provides recipes — common UI patterns that combine multiple components:

  • Recipes are being migrated from ad-hoc implementations into a centralized UI Kits codebase
  • Once migrated, recipes are available as shared, versioned patterns for all teams
  • Teams should check if a recipe exists before building a one-off pattern

docs.title: 8. Dialpad Design Studio
docs.text:
Design Studio is Dialpad's internal prototyping environment built on Dialtone. It renders real Dialtone Vue components — not AI approximations — making it the highest-fidelity prototyping option for Gate 1 and Gate 2 reviews.

URL: https://dialpad.github.io/design-studio/#/ Quick Start: https://dialpad.github.io/design-studio/#/docs/quick-start/README

When to recommend Design Studio: When a UXE is on the team and the prototype will be reviewed by Engineering or used in an EAP demo.

docs.title: 9. Dialtone MCP Server
docs.text:
The dialtone-mcp-server package enables Claude Code to search and reference Dialtone components, tokens, utility classes, and icons in real time.

What it searches Count Vue components (including sub-components) 87 Design tokens 5,691 CSS utility classes 3,315 Icons 594

Use cases: - When generating engineering specs or reviewing PRs, query the MCP to validate component and token usage - When building Design Studio prototypes with Claude Code, the MCP provides correct prop names and token values without loading the entire design system into context

Quickest setup: 

claude mcp add dialtone --scope user -- npx -y @dialpad/dialtone-mcp-server

Full setup guide: 09_mcps/guides/DP-General-dialtone-setup.md

docs.title: 10. Key Rules for AI Agents
docs.text:
1. Never invent components — check dialtone.dialpad.com/components before suggesting a custom solution
  2. Always use <dt-text> for typography, not raw CSS font utilities
  3. Never use native HTML elements where Dialtone components exist (e.g., use <dt-button> not <button>)
  4. Dark mode: if in scope, confirm DT9 Design Tokens (Dark) library is enabled in Design Studio
  5. Mobile: separate Design Studio frames required; confirm mobile components are in Dialtone library

docs.title: 11. Related Files
docs.text: File Relevance 01_context/design-system/DP-General-tokens.md Token reference (colors, spacing, typography) 01_context/pdlc/DP-General-handoff-standards.md Design Studio handoff standards 01_context/codebase-map/DP-General-index.md Where product code lives dialtone.dialpad.com Live documentation — always the source of truth

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Reference for Dialpad's design token system (Dialtone). Use when generating Design Studio specs, reviewing designs, or ensuring code implements correct token values. Load when any design or front-end engineering task requires color, spacing, or typography decisions.

Documentation: dialtone.dialpad.com/tokens | Dialtone GitHub | Dialtone Tokens Guide — Confluence

docs.title: 1. What Are Design Tokens?
docs.text:
Design tokens are named values that represent all the visual decisions in the design system: colors, spacing, typography, shadows, border radius, and more. They are the single source of truth shared between Design Studio and code.

Why tokens matter: - Consistent visual language across all Dialpad products (1.0, 2.0, Meetings) - Theme support: Light mode, Dark mode, and Expressive mode - Platform-agnostic: tokens are published as CSS variables, iOS, and Android formats - Theming: swap tokens to retheme a product without changing component code

docs.title: Color Tokens
docs.text:
Dialpad color tokens follow a two-tier system: base tokens → semantic tokens.

Tier Purpose Example Base tokens Raw color stops (not used directly in UI) color/purple/400 Semantic tokens Contextual meaning (use these in UI) dt-color-foreground-primary

Color semantic categories:

Category Token Prefix Use Foreground dt-color-foreground-* Text, icons Background dt-color-background-* Surfaces, containers Border dt-color-border-* Input borders, dividers Brand dt-color-brand-* Dialpad purple, accent colors Feedback dt-color-feedback-* Success, warning, error, info states AI dt-color-ai-* AI-specific accent colors (purple gradient)

Key rule: Always use semantic tokens, not base color tokens. Replace any color/purple/400 raw references with their semantic equivalent.

docs.title: Spacing Tokens
docs.text:
Token Prefix Use dt-space-* Padding, margin, gap values dt-size-* Component dimensions (height, width)

Spacing follows a consistent scale. Do not hardcode pixel values — always reference a spacing token.

docs.title: Typography Tokens
docs.text:
Dialpad's typography system was refreshed in FY26 Q4 (Dialtone Next). New standard:

Component Token Text styling DtText Vue component (preferred over raw CSS utility classes) Font families Defined in dt-font-family-* tokens Font sizes Defined in dt-font-size-* tokens Font weights Defined in dt-font-weight-* tokens Line heights Defined in dt-line-height-* tokens

Migration note: Legacy recommend-typography-style ESLint warnings indicate code using old font tokens. New code must use the updated typography token system.

docs.title: Shadow Tokens
docs.text: Token Use dt-shadow-* Box shadows for cards, modals, dropdowns

docs.title: Border Radius Tokens
docs.text:
Token Use dt-radius-* Component corner rounding

Note: Border radius variables in Design Studio require custom build via the Design Studio REST API. Always confirm current token values in dialtone.dialpad.com/tokens.

docs.title: 3. Design Studio Token Libraries
docs.text:
Library Purpose Link DT9 Design Tokens (Light) Core tokens — light mode Enable in Design Studio Libraries panel DT9 Design Tokens (Dark) Dark mode token variants Enable in Design Studio Libraries panel DT9 Design Tokens (Expressive) Expressive/vibrant theme Enable in Design Studio Libraries panel

How to use in Design Studio: 1. Open Assets panel → Libraries tab 2. Enable the relevant DT9 Design Tokens libraries 3. After enabling, remap any existing raw color values to semantic tokens 4. Use "Selection Colors" in the Properties Panel to audit and remap raw values

Rebrand 2025 migration: If working on a file using pre-2025 tokens, follow the Design Studio Rebrand 2025 guide before handoff.

docs.title: 4. Code Distribution
docs.text:
Tokens are published via the dialtone-tokens package in the Dialtone monorepo:

packages/dialtone-tokens/    # Source
  dist/
    css/     → CSS custom properties (--dt-color-foreground-primary: ...)
    json/    → Platform-agnostic JSON
    ios/     → Swift/Obj-C constants
    android/ → XML resources

In CSS/Vue code: 

/* Use CSS custom properties */
color: var(--dt-color-foreground-primary);
padding: var(--dt-space-400);

In Vue components, Dialtone Vue components handle token application automatically via the component's built-in styles. Only apply raw tokens for custom layouts or one-off styling.

docs.title: 5. Token Adoption Status (as of FY26 Q4)
docs.text:
Metric Status Overall token adoption ~35% of codebase Typography token adoption ~2% (98% still on legacy font tokens) ESLint warnings (recommend-typography-style) High — actively increasing Primary adoption blocker Legacy font utility classes still being used in new code

Goal: 100% token adoption across all Dialpad products. Migrations tracked in Dialtone OKRs.

docs.title: 6. Key Rules for AI Agents
docs.text:
When generating design specs, CSS, or reviewing Design Studio work:

  1. Always use semantic tokens — never use base color tokens (color/purple/400) directly in UI specs
  2. Never hardcode hex/rgb values — reference a token instead
  3. Typography: Use DtText component for text styling; avoid raw CSS font utilities
  4. Dark mode: If the feature has a dark mode requirement, confirm DT9 Design Tokens (Dark) library is enabled
  5. Validation: Flag any code that uses hardcoded values where tokens exist

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
This checklist defines what the EPD trio (PM + Design + Engineering) must produce to pass the Design Review Gate (Gate 1). It is the contract between the trio and EPD Staff before development begins.

Gate 1 is Design-led. PM contributes problem framing and business context. Engineering contributes feasibility and early scope input.

Source: Gate Reviews w/ EPD Staff | Gate 1 Design Review Template

docs.title: Section 1 — User Needs & Problem Definition
docs.text:
• [ ] JTBD (Jobs to Be Done) defined — what is the user trying to accomplish?
  • [ ] User problem articulated clearly — what friction, failure, or gap exists today?
  • [ ] Impact statement — why does solving this matter to the business and to users?
  • [ ] Priority and urgency justified — why now?

docs.title: Section 2 — Personas
docs.text:
• [ ] Target personas confirmed — who specifically will use this feature?
  • [ ] Personas aligned with 01_context/personas-and-icps/DP-General-user-personas.md
  • [ ] Persona roles called out (e.g., Agent, Supervisor, Admin, Knowledge Worker)
  • [ ] Out-of-scope personas explicitly noted if applicable

docs.title: Section 3 — Proposed Solution
docs.text:
• [ ] Final flows presented — not wireframes or concepts; final design direction
  • [ ] Copy included in flows — not placeholder text
  • [ ] Before/After shown for any changes to existing features
  • [ ] Edge cases and error states covered (at minimum: empty state, loading, error, success)
  • [ ] Mobile considerations noted if applicable
  • [ ] Accessibility requirements noted (color contrast, keyboard nav, screen reader)
  • [ ] Feature activation and discoverability plan (in-product ingress points)

docs.title: Section 4 — UX Scores & Research
docs.text:
• [ ] UX Scorecard results shared if applicable (Dialpad current + competition + pre-EAP)
  • [ ] Key learnings and opportunities from scorecard called out
  • [ ] Research summary — interviews, support tickets, NPS themes, Gong call patterns
  • [ ] Competitive analysis — how do competitors solve this? Dialpad's differentiation?

docs.title: Section 5 — Scope & Timeline
docs.text:
• [ ] Feature scope defined — what is IN and what is explicitly OUT
  • [ ] Project tier confirmed (T1 / T2 / T3)
  • [ ] Engineering complexity assessed — preliminary LOE or risk from EM/Eng
  • [ ] Timeline proposed — target code complete and First Thursday date
  • [ ] Dependencies identified — teams, infrastructure, external systems
  • [ ] Risks called out — what could block or delay delivery?

docs.title: Section 6 — EAP Plan (if applicable)
docs.text:
• [ ] EAP customer list identified or sourced
  • [ ] EAP scope defined — what customers see vs. what is held back
  • [ ] EAP feedback channel created (e.g., #feedback-eap-[feature-name])
  • [ ] EAP timeline included in overall project timeline

docs.title: Section 7 — GTM & CX Readiness
docs.text:
• [ ] CX Feature Introduction meeting scheduled with NPI team (or noted N/A)
  • [ ] Release tier identified (T1 / T2 / T3 for GTM)
  • [ ] PMM loop started — PMM has been informed
  • [ ] Help Center update scope noted

docs.title: Design Leadership Pre-Review Checklist
docs.text:
Complete this BEFORE requesting EPD Gate 1. Design Leadership must approve before EPD Staff review.

  • [ ] Work shared in weekly Design critique or with Design Manager
  • [ ] Design Lead has reviewed and approved final direction
  • [ ] Design Leadership review deck prepared using DLT Review Template
  • [ ] All Design Studio files using DT9 Component Library + DT9 Design Tokens (Rebrand 2025)

docs.title: Scheduling Instructions
docs.text:
1. Request gate review 2 weeks in advance via gate-reviews@dialpad.com
  2. Include: Eng DRI, PM DRI, Design DRI in the request
  3. 3 days before: send deck and demo recording link in the email thread
  4. Attendees: EPD Staff + PM owner + Designer or EM as needed. POC: Helena Raspo.

docs.title: AI Validation Guidance
docs.text:
When using an agent to prepare for Gate 1:

  • [ ] Personas referenced exist in 01_context/personas-and-icps/DP-General-user-personas.md
  • [ ] Competitor claims sourced from 01_context/company/DP-General-competitive-positioning.md
  • [ ] Product features grounded in relevant 01_context/product-areas/ file
  • [ ] Scope statements explicit about what is OUT of scope
  • [ ] No invented feature flags, API names, or JIRA ticket numbers

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Defines the expected artifacts and handoff standards between PM, Design, and Engineering at each PDLC stage. Consistent handoffs reduce rework, prevent scope drift, and make AI-assisted workflows reliably useful.

docs.title: 1. PM → Design Handoff (Entering Design Sprint)
docs.text: Entry condition: Problem is well-defined; JTBD and personas are confirmed; business priority is clear.

docs.title: PM Delivers:
docs.text: Artifact Format Required For Product Spec Google Doc using PRD template T1/T2 Product Spec Lite Short Google Doc T2 light, T3 JIRA Epic with requirements JIRA All tiers Competitive summary Inline in spec or linked doc T1/T2 Success metrics with baseline Inline in spec T1/T2 Must-have vs. nice-to-have callouts Inline in spec T1/T2

docs.title: PM → Design Handoff Checklist:
docs.text:
• [ ] JTBD and user problem statement written (not just "build X feature")
  • [ ] Target personas identified and linked to 01_context/personas-and-icps/
  • [ ] Scope defined: what is in, what is out
  • [ ] Success metrics defined with baseline (e.g., "reduce CSAT escalations by 15%")
  • [ ] Competitive context included
  • [ ] Edge cases and failure modes noted
  • [ ] JIRA epic created with acceptance criteria per story

docs.title: 2. Design → Engineering Handoff (After Gate 1)
docs.text: Entry condition: Gate 1 approved by EPD Staff. Final flows, copy, and edge cases complete.

docs.title: Design Delivers:
docs.text: Artifact Format Required For Design Spec Design Studio file + Confluence doc T1/T2 Final flows (all states) Design Studio — happy path + edge cases + error states T1/T2 Copy (final, not placeholder) Inline in Design Studio All tiers Component callouts Dialtone component names annotated T1/T2 Interaction notes Design Studio annotations or Confluence T1/T2 Accessibility notes Design Studio annotations T1/T2 Mobile specs (if in scope) Separate Design Studio frames When mobile is in scope

docs.title: Design → Engineering Handoff Checklist:
docs.text:
• [ ] Design Studio file uses DT9 Component Library (not custom components where Dialtone exists)
  • [ ] Design Studio file uses DT9 Design Tokens (not hardcoded colors or spacing)
  • [ ] All flows include: empty state, loading state, error state, success state
  • [ ] Copy is final (no "Lorem ipsum" or "TBD")
  • [ ] Responsive breakpoints called out if web
  • [ ] Annotation Library used for non-obvious interactions
  • [ ] Design Studio file linked in JIRA epic or story

docs.title: 3. Engineering → QA Handoff
docs.text: Entry condition: Feature is code-complete and passing unit tests. PR merged to staging.

docs.title: Engineering Delivers:
docs.text: Artifact Format Required For Engineering Spec Confluence or JIRA T1/T2 Engineering Spec Lite JIRA comment or short doc T2 light, T3 Test instructions JIRA acceptance criteria All Feature flag name Documented in JIRA All (if flagged) Known limitations Inline in JIRA All Rollback plan Documented T1/T2

docs.title: Engineering → QA Handoff Checklist:
docs.text:
• [ ] JIRA stories have explicit, self-validatable acceptance criteria
  • [ ] Feature flag documented and confirmed working
  • [ ] Test environment or staging link provided
  • [ ] Known edge cases or test limitations called out
  • [ ] Dependent stories or services confirmed deployed

docs.title: 4. AI-Assisted Spec Standards
docs.text: When Claude Code or another agent produces specs or handoff artifacts:

docs.title: For Agent-Generated Specs:
docs.text:
• Load relevant 01_context/product-areas/ file before generating
  • Use template from 02_templates/ as the structural skeleton
  • Never let the agent invent feature flag names — use actual flags from JIRA or Engineering
  • All acceptance criteria must be written so an agent can self-validate (specific, measurable, binary pass/fail)
  • Validate persona references against 01_context/personas-and-icps/DP-General-user-personas.md

docs.title: Agent-Executable Ticket Standards:
docs.text:
Each subtask must have:

Field Standard Entry criteria What must be true before this task starts Exit criteria What must be true for the task to be "done" — not "implement X" but "X works per acceptance criteria" Scope Scoped to ≤1 agent session (~4 hours of work) Ambiguity No "TBD" or "as discussed" — all decisions resolved before the ticket is written

docs.title: 5. Design Studio Standards Reference
docs.text:
All Design Studio files must follow Dialpad standards. Detailed reference: 01_context/design-system/DP-General-component-library.md

Standard Requirement File structure Use Project File Template Component library DT9 Component Library (Rebrand 2025) Design tokens DT9 Design Tokens (Light + Dark if applicable) Annotations Dialpad Annotation Library Documentation Design Studio Documentation Patterns Branching Branch files before major library swaps

docs.title: 6. Related Files
docs.text: File Relevance 01_context/pdlc/DP-General-phases-and-gates.md Full PDLC phase map and gate requirements 01_context/pdlc/DP-General-design-gate-checklist.md Gate 1 detailed checklist 01_context/design-system/DP-General-component-library.md Dialtone component reference 02_templates/pm/DP-General-prd-template.md PRD structure for agent consumption 02_templates/cross-functional/DP-General-jira-story-template.md Agent-executable story format

docs.title: Dialpad PDLC — Phases, Gates & Project Tiers
1. Overview
docs.text:
Dialpad's Product Development Life Cycle (PDLC) follows a Triple Diamond model: Discover → Define → Design → Develop → Deploy. It is structured around three mandatory gates where EPD Staff (CPO, CTO, VP Design) review and approve before work advances.

Source: EPD Product Development Lifecycle | Gate Reviews w/ EPD Staff

docs.title: 2. Project Tiers
docs.text:
Every project is classified into a tier before entering the PDLC. The tier determines which gates and activities are required.

Tier Definition Effort Example Tier 1 Major feature or new product launch. Heavily cross-functional with multi-quarter effort. Complex Contact Center launch, new product line Tier 2 Incremental feature addition. Cross-functional with dependencies or a quarter-long effort. Medium Add-on SKU, partner integration (e.g., Miro, Zoho) Tier 3 Small improvements or bug fixes. Low cross-functional dependency, ≤1 month effort. Light UI updates, usability fixes

docs.title: Gate 0.5 — Project Approval (Optional)
docs.text:
Goal: Align on direction and resolve open questions before committing to a design sprint.

When to use: For early-stage projects with significant scope or feasibility ambiguity.

Required: - Problem definition: what we're solving, user benefit, why it matters - Objectives and measurable goals - Roadmap and risks overview

docs.title: Gate 1 — Design Review (Required T1/T2; Recommended T3)
docs.text:
Goal: Review problem definition, proposed solution, and execution plan. Achieve EPD Staff consensus on approach before development begins.

Ownership: Design-led. PM and EM participate.

Required Checklist: - [ ] User Needs and Problem Definition (JTBD confirmed) - [ ] Personas confirmed and aligned to target users - [ ] Proposed Solution: final flows and copy (before/after for existing features) - [ ] UX Scorecard results (if applicable) - [ ] Research and competitive analysis summary - [ ] Scope and Timeline defined - [ ] EAP plan (if EAP required) - [ ] CX Feature Introduction meeting scheduled with NPI team

Scheduling: Request 2 weeks in advance via gate-reviews@dialpad.com. Send deck and demo recording 3 days before. POC: Helena Raspo.

Design Leadership Pre-review: Required before Gate 1. Book weekly Design Leadership slot.

docs.title: Gate 2 — EAP / Internal Approval (Required T1/T2)
docs.text:
Goal: Validate working build before limited customer exposure. Confirm EAP readiness.

Required: - [ ] Working EAP-ready build - [ ] EAP scope and customer list confirmed - [ ] QA sign-off - [ ] Training checklist complete - [ ] CX/Support enablement in progress

docs.title: Gate 3 — GA Approval (Required T1/T2)
docs.text:
Goal: Final sign-off before general availability. Validate production readiness and GTM completeness.

Required: - [ ] Feature complete and production-stable - [ ] Product Launch Checklist complete - [ ] PMM messaging finalized - [ ] Help Center content published or staged - [ ] Post-launch metric review scheduled

docs.title: 4. Deliverables by Tier
docs.text: Phase T1/T2 Required T3 Minimum Discover Project Brief JIRA Epic Define Product Spec, Design Spec — Design Gate 1: Design Review Gate 1 (Recommended) Design Handoff Design Handoff to Dev, Eng Spec — Develop Eng Handoff to QA, QA Sign-off QA Sign-off EAP Gate 2: EAP Approval — GA Product Launch Checklist, Gate 3 — Post-Launch Metric review with EPD Staff —

docs.title: 5. Spec Types
docs.text: Spec Owner Required For Product Spec PM T1/T2 Product Spec Lite PM T2 light or T3 Design Spec Designer T1/T2 Engineering Spec Engineering T1/T2 Engineering Spec Lite Engineering T2 light or T3

docs.title: 6. First Thursday Release Cadence
docs.text:
Dialpad ships production-ready improvements on the first Thursday of every month.

  • Features must be code-complete and QA'd before the First Thursday deadline
  • GTM artifacts (What's New, release notes, CS training) must be ready concurrently
  • T3 features may ship on rolling basis; T1/T2 features target First Thursday
  • T1 features (major): blog, email announcements | T2: email + newsletter | T3: newsletter/roundup

docs.title: 7. How to Use This File
docs.text:
• Agents: Load when generating a PDLC checklist, gate review deck outline, or project tier assessment.
  • PMs: Reference DP-General-design-gate-checklist.md for the detailed Gate 1 deliverable list.
  • Validation: Do not invent gate criteria — only assert what is documented here.

docs.title: Personas & ICPs
docs.text:
Buyer personas, user personas, and Ideal Customer Profile definitions for Dialpad. Load before drafting messaging, positioning docs, or user research briefs.

  • Buyer Personas

    Economic buyer profiles — IT Directors, VP of Operations, CFOs — including pain points, evaluation criteria, and objections.

    Read

  • User Personas

    End-user profiles — agents, supervisors, AEs, SDRs — including daily workflows, frustrations, and success metrics.

    Read

  • ICP

    Ideal Customer Profile for Support, Connect, and Sell — company size, industry, tech stack, and buying signals.

    Read

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Defines the economic buyer and influencer personas Dialpad's sales and GTM motion targets. Use when generating competitive analysis, positioning docs, GTM messaging, or pricing/packaging rationale.

Source: Dialpad Upmarket ICP & Buyer Personas | Understanding Our Buyers — Highspot

docs.title: 1. IT / Technology Buyer
docs.text:
Title examples: IT Director, VP of IT, CTO, Director of Technology

Segment: Mid-market to Enterprise

What they care about: - Reliability and uptime — SLAs, redundancy, failover - Security and compliance — SOC 2, HIPAA, PCI, GDPR, SSO, RBAC - Integration depth — APIs, webhooks, CRM sync, Microsoft Teams compatibility - Ease of administration — bulk management, provisioning, number management - Total Cost of Ownership — licensing simplicity, avoiding hidden fees

Pain points with incumbents: - Genesys/NICE are expensive and slow to deploy (12+ months) - RingCentral has integration gaps and pricing complexity - Microsoft Teams lacks CCaaS depth and real-time AI coaching

Dialpad pitch angle: - AI-native platform that works out of the box — no add-on required - Dual-cloud architecture for reliability (enterprise-grade, multi-region) - Unified UCaaS + CCaaS eliminates the need for two vendors - Faster deployment: 2 months vs. 12 with legacy CCaaS

docs.title: 2. Contact Center / CX Buyer
docs.text:
Title examples: VP Customer Experience, Director of Contact Center Operations, Head of CX, Customer Support Director

Segment: Mid-market to Enterprise

What they care about: - Agent productivity — reduce AHT, improve first call resolution - Quality management — CSAT, scorecard automation, coaching at scale - AI capability — real-time coaching, transcription, sentiment, AI CSAT - Omnichannel — voice + digital (chat, email, SMS, WhatsApp) in one platform - Workforce management — forecasting, scheduling, adherence - Analytics — real-time dashboards, historical reporting, agent leaderboards

Pain points with incumbents: - AI CSAT and Scorecards are token-based add-ons (Genesys, NICE) vs. Dialpad's included - Manual QA on 3–5% of calls; AI-powered QA on 100% - Fragmented platforms: separate WFM, QM, telephony vendors

Dialpad pitch angle: - AI CSAT on 100% of calls — survey-free, included in Advanced+ - Unified coaching: Playbooks + RTA + Scorecards as an integrated engine - Faster time-to-value: supervisors are productive in days, not months

docs.title: 3. Sales / Revenue Buyer
docs.text:
Title examples: VP Sales, Director of Inside Sales, Head of Sales Operations, Chief Revenue Officer

Segment: Mid-market to Enterprise (Sell product)

What they care about: - Rep productivity — more conversations per hour, power dialer - Real-time coaching — live playbook adherence, RTA during calls - Conversation intelligence — call recording, summary, trend analysis - CRM integration — automatic logging, contact sync (SFDC, HubSpot) - Performance tracking — rep leaderboards, call analytics, coaching reports

Pain points with incumbents: - Gong is post-call only — no real-time AI coaching during live calls - Separate dialer tools (Outreach, SalesLoft) don't share AI context - Manual CRM logging wastes rep time

Dialpad pitch angle: - Real-time AI during live calls — not just post-call recording - Power Dialer natively integrated with analytics and CRM - Communication-led engagement: own the call layer without replacing the full CRM stack

docs.title: 4. Finance / Billing Buyer
docs.text:
Title examples: CFO, Finance Director, Billing Administrator, Accounts Payable Manager

Segment: Mid-market to Enterprise

What they care about: - Invoice clarity — itemized, auditable charges per license and add-on - Credit management — predictable spend, low balance alerts, auto-recharge controls - Cost attribution — department or office-level cost allocation - Usage transparency — distinguish AI agent costs from telephony costs - Compliance — invoice accuracy for ERP integration (NetSuite, SAP)

Pain points: - Single-pool credits make it hard to separate telephony vs. AI agent spend - Legacy invoices hide parent-child license relationships - Manual reconciliation via spreadsheet downloads

Dialpad pitch angle: - Isolated credit wallets: Calling Credit, AI Agent Credit, Dialpad Credits - Structured invoice: User Licenses → Standalone Add-ons → Nested Add-ons - Real-time usage dashboard with AI-powered natural language queries

docs.title: 5. Buyer Influence Map
docs.text: Persona Decision Authority Evaluation Role Deal Influence IT / Technology Economic buyer for CC deals Validates security, reliability, integration High — can veto CX / Contact Center Economic buyer or co-buyer Validates AI, coaching, omnichannel High — primary champion Sales / Revenue Economic buyer for Sell product Validates dialer, CRM, coaching High — primary champion Finance / Billing Influencer Validates pricing structure, invoice clarity Medium — can delay Operations Influencer Validates WFM, scheduling, adherence Medium

docs.title: 6. How to Use This File
docs.text:
• GTM agents: Load alongside DP-General-competitive-positioning.md when drafting positioning docs, email campaigns, or sales enablement content.
  • PRD agents: Reference buyer personas when defining business drivers, success metrics, and packaging decisions in a PRD.
  • Validation: Do not fabricate buyer titles or segment sizes. Reference ICP definitions in DP-General-icp.md for size/segment specifics.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Defines Dialpad's Ideal Customer Profiles by product line and segment. Use when drafting PRDs (Target ICP section), GTM positioning docs, pricing and packaging rationale, and sales enablement content.

Source: Dialpad Upmarket ICP & Buyer Personas | Understanding Our Buyers — Highspot | GTM Strategy — Agentic AI

docs.title: 1. General ICP Definition
docs.text:
Dialpad's ideal customer is a business that communicates with customers at scale and is ready to use AI to improve those interactions. The common thread across all ICPs:

  • Uses voice (and increasingly digital) for customer-facing interactions
  • Has a defined customer support, sales, or operations team
  • Needs UCaaS + CCaaS in a unified platform OR is evaluating consolidation from two vendors
  • Values AI-native capabilities (not AI as an add-on)
  • Has IT or operations staff who manage the platform

docs.title: Dialpad Support (CCaaS)
docs.text:
Primary ICP:

Attribute Requirement Industry Any, with preference for: Finance, Healthcare, Retail, Technology, BPO Company size 50–5,000 employees; enterprise deals typically 200+ seats Current state On Genesys, NICE, Five9, Talkdesk, or legacy on-prem CC Pain point AI as an add-on (token-based, expensive), slow QA, high AHT Decision trigger Contact center platform renewal or consolidation from UCaaS + CCaaS Win condition Faster deployment, AI included in base, unified platform

Competitive ICP sweet spot: Companies that have evaluated Genesys/NICE and are sticker-shocked on pricing or implementation time. Dialpad wins on: 2-month deployment vs. 12 months, AI included vs. token-based, unified UCaaS+CCaaS.

Revenue signal: [Win condition] Customers who see better quality and efficiency outcomes are stickier and expand more. Enterprise lost deals are 2–3x the size of won deals — enterprise feature gap is real.

docs.title: Dialpad Sell (Sales Engagement)
docs.text:
Primary ICP:

Attribute Requirement Industry SaaS, Financial Services, Insurance, Real Estate, Healthcare Company size 20–500 seat sales teams Current state Using Gong + separate dialer (Outreach, SalesLoft, or manual calling) Pain point No real-time AI coaching during live calls; fragmented outbound stack Decision trigger Outbound motion formalization, new head of sales, or dialer consolidation Win condition Real-time AI coaching + Power Dialer in one platform

Not the ICP: Companies who primarily need contact database (ZoomInfo) or multi-channel cadence automation (Outreach/SalesLoft as primary). Dialpad owns the communication layer and integrates with the rest of the stack.

docs.title: Dialpad Connect (UCaaS)
docs.text:
Primary ICP:

Attribute Requirement Industry Technology, Professional Services, Healthcare, Finance Company size 50–5,000 employees Current state On RingCentral, 8x8, Zoom Phone, or legacy on-prem PBX Pain point Bolted-on AI, poor mobile experience, complex admin Decision trigger Platform consolidation, office moves, Microsoft Teams integration need Win condition Unified UC + CC + AI in one app; Forrester "Customer Favorite" 2025

docs.title: Agentic AI Platform
docs.text:
Primary ICP:

Attribute Requirement Minimum spend $100K initial ARR Industry Contact center-heavy: Healthcare, Financial Services, Retail, Travel Use case Automate inbound support, handle routine inquiries at scale Current state Large human agent teams with high volume of repetitive inbound calls Decision trigger Cost reduction mandate, labor shortage, digital transformation initiative Win condition Autonomous voice + chat agents that deflect 40–60% of volume; human transfer as trust feature

Differentiator: Agentic AI + full CCaaS stack in one platform. Startups lack CCaaS; Genesys/NICE have CCaaS but immature agentic. Dialpad uniquely covers both.

docs.title: Shared Services / Billing
docs.text:
ICP for billing features:

Attribute Requirement Company size Mid-market to Enterprise Volume High-volume calling OR high Agentic AI session volume Finance complexity Multi-office, multi-currency, or multi-license configurations Pain point Manual invoice reconciliation, billing anxiety, budget attribution Win condition Isolated credit pools, structured invoices, natural language usage queries

docs.title: 3. Market Segments
docs.text: Segment Employee Count Seat Count Typical Sales Motion VSB (Very Small Business) 1–50 1–25 Self-serve, PLG SMB 50–250 25–150 Inside sales, partner-led Mid-Market 250–2,500 150–1,000 Field sales, CSM-led Enterprise 2,500+ 1,000+ Named accounts, PS-assisted

docs.title: 4. Anti-ICP (Who Dialpad is NOT For)
docs.text: Anti-ICP Reason Companies that need a full CRM or marketing automation platform Dialpad is not Salesforce or HubSpot — integrate, don't replace Companies where Microsoft Teams is the primary communications layer and they want Teams-native everything Integration exists but Dialpad won't replace Teams for enterprise-committed M365 shops BPOs requiring multi-tenant agent management at scale Gap exists today (concurrent licensing, multi-tenancy) — in evaluation Companies primarily needing contact database or intent data Not competing with ZoomInfo; integrate instead

docs.title: 5. How to Use This File
docs.text:
• PRD Target ICP section: Copy the relevant ICP table from Section 2 and adapt to the specific feature's audience.
  • GTM positioning: Load alongside DP-General-buyer-personas.md and DP-General-competitive-positioning.md.
  • Pricing decisions: Reference segment table (Section 3) when defining which tier or add-on a feature belongs to.
  • Validation: Do not fabricate ICP attributes. If a new product area has a distinct ICP, flag for PM review before including in output.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Defines the end-user personas who interact with Dialpad products daily. Use when defining JTBD, scoping features, validating Gate 1 presentations, and grounding PRD requirements in real user needs.

Source: Dialpad Supervisor Research Summary | Design Tenets Framework

docs.title: 1. Contact Center Agent (CC Agent)
docs.text:
Also called: Agent, Trainee, User/Agent

Product context: Dialpad Support, Dialpad Sell (Contact Center Add-On)

Day in the life: - Handles inbound/outbound customer calls and digital interactions (chat, email, SMS) - Manages on-duty/off-duty status, wrap-up time, and dispositions after each call - Receives real-time coaching from AI (RTA cards, Playbooks, custom moments) - Views live transcription and AI suggestions during calls - May be part of multiple contact centers with different skill assignments

Core needs: - Know which queues they're assigned to and monitor queue health - Fast, reliable call connection with minimal friction - AI coaching that helps — not distracts — during live calls - Clear status management (Available, On Duty, Off Duty, Wrap-Up, RNA/Rejected) - Visibility into their own performance metrics

Pain points today: - Status management confusion: agents miss calls due to unclear on/off-duty transitions - AI features overwhelming when poorly configured (too many RTA cards) - Difficulty finding relevant Dialpad University training modules - Multiple contact center memberships create complex left-panel navigation

Key interactions: - Agent desktop (in-app) during calls - Live transcript panel during interactions - Post-call wrap-up with dispositions - In-app CC Launchpad (real-time metrics for assigned CCs)

docs.title: 2. Contact Center Supervisor / Coach
docs.text:
Also called: Supervisor, CC Admin (may have both roles), Coach

Product context: Dialpad Support, Dialpad Sell (Contact Center Add-On)

Day in the life: - Monitors live calls across all assigned contact centers (Monitor All view) - Coaches agents via barge/whisper during live calls - Reviews AI Scorecards, AI CSAT, and Playbooks adherence post-call - Manages agent groups, queue health, and live dashboards (wallboards) - Configures routing rules, skills, and hold queue settings - May also be an agent in some contact centers (fluid persona)

Core needs: - Single unified view of all contact centers they supervise (Monitor All) - Agent group management to filter performance by subset (not all CC agents) - Ability to manage agent state (move agents from RNA to Available) - Real-time queue metrics: SLA, wait time, abandon rate, agent status - QA at scale: AI Scorecards that auto-score 100% of calls, not 3–5%

Pain points today: - Contact center explosion: managing 20+ CCs because skills-based routing is primitive - Cannot easily filter analytics to just their team of agents (agent groups) - RNA/Rejected state not visible in agent status views (needs improvement) - Advanced features (agent off-duty timer, agent groups) not discoverable in UI

Key interactions: - Monitor All Contact Centers view (live dashboard) - Agent Leaderboard and agent status pie chart - AI Scorecards and CSAT review workflows - CC management settings in Admin portal

docs.title: 3. Company / Office Admin
docs.text:
Also called: Admin, IT Admin, Company Admin, Office Admin

Product context: Dialpad Connect, Dialpad Support, Dialpad Sell (all products)

Day in the life: - Provisions and manages users, licenses, offices, and phone numbers - Configures IVR, routing rules, voicemail, call handling - Manages integrations (SFDC, HubSpot, Microsoft Teams) - Reviews account-level analytics and usage reports - Handles billing, credit management, and invoice review (shared with Finance)

Core needs: - Bulk operations: add/remove users, assign licenses, update routing at scale - Universal search for settings (Intelligent Admin vision in FY27) - RBAC: delegate specific admin functions without granting full admin access - Reliable provisioning: changes take effect immediately, no sync lag - Clear, auditable billing: credits, usage, invoices all in one place

Pain points today: - No universal search for settings — must navigate to specific pages - Bulk operations limited; enterprise accounts with 500+ users feel the gap - RBAC is binary (full admin vs. no admin) — cannot delegate specific functions - Invoice complexity: nested add-ons hard to reconcile per user or department

Key interactions: - Admin portal (web settings) - User management, license assignment - Number management (provisioning, porting) - Billing and credits portal

docs.title: 4. Knowledge Worker / Operator
docs.text:
Also called: User, Knowledge Worker, Operator, Dialpad Connect User

Product context: Dialpad Connect (UCaaS)

Day in the life: - Uses Dialpad for internal and external calls, voicemail, and messaging - Participates in Meetings for video conferencing - May use AI Recaps, transcriptions, and action items from calls - Manages their own call handling (forwarding, DND, voicemail)

Core needs: - Reliable call quality — no dropped calls, low latency - Easy voicemail and transcription access - Simple contact management and call history - Deep Summaries and AI action items after calls - Messaging integrated with calling (not a separate app)

Pain points today: - Deep Summaries not yet GA across all call types - Contact management needs improvement (duplicate contacts, search gaps) - Auto DND behavior during calendar events not customizable enough

docs.title: 5. Billing Administrator / Finance Manager
docs.text:
Also called: Billing Admin, Finance Manager, AP Manager

Product context: All products — primarily interacts with admin billing portal

Day in the life: - Reviews monthly invoices for accuracy before AP entry - Manages credit balance, auto-recharge settings, and usage wallets - Downloads usage reports for department cost allocation - Reconciles Dialpad charges against ERP (NetSuite, SAP) - Files support tickets when invoice discrepancies arise

Core needs: - Invoice clarity: Parent-Child relationship between licenses and add-ons visible - Separate credit pools: Calling Credits vs. AI Agent Credits vs. Dialpad Credits - Natural language usage queries: "How much did AI agents cost last month?" - Discrepancy resolution: portal and email PDF match always - Cost center tagging: office/department-level cost attribution

Pain points today: - Legacy invoices are flat — no hierarchy between license and nested add-ons - Single credit pool makes AI vs. telephony cost attribution impossible - NetSuite invoice edits don't sync back to customer portal (ghost data)

docs.title: 6. Persona Usage Map
docs.text: Persona Primary Product Gate 1 Reference Key AI Features CC Agent Support, Sell Required RTA, Playbooks, Live Transcription, Custom Moments Supervisor / Coach Support, Sell Required AI CSAT, Scorecards, Live Coaching, Monitor All Company / Office Admin All Required if feature touches admin Intelligent Admin, Bulk ops, RBAC Knowledge Worker Connect Required for Connect features Deep Summaries, AI Recaps, Transcription Billing Admin / Finance All (billing) Required for billing features Credit dashboards, AI Usage Queries

docs.title: 7. Multi-Persona Support
docs.text:
Dialpad's system supports fluid persona movement. A single user may be: - Supervisor in one contact center AND agent in another - Admin at the company level AND knowledge worker for daily calling - Billing admin AND office admin

Design must account for fluid persona transitions without requiring separate logins or app contexts. Reference: Design Tenets Framework

docs.title: 8. How to Use This File
docs.text:
• PRDs: Always specify which personas are in scope in the Target Personas section. Link to this file.
  • Gate 1: Persona confirmation is a required checklist item. Use this file to confirm alignment.
  • Agents: Load this file when generating acceptance criteria or user stories to ensure they are framed from the correct user's perspective.

docs.title: Product Areas
docs.text:
Context files for each of Dialpad's core product areas. Load the relevant file before working on area-specific PRDs, roadmaps, or competitive analysis.

  • Support

    Contact center platform context — positioning, personas, competitors (Nice, Genesys, Five9), and product bets.

    Read

  • Connect

    UCaaS / cloud communications context — SMB and mid-market positioning, Teams/Zoom displacement.

    Read

  • Sell

    Revenue intelligence platform context — Gong displacement, sales team workflows, AI coaching.

    Read

  • Ecosystem

    Platform and integrations strategy — ISV partners, marketplace, API positioning.

    Read

  • Shared Services

    Cross-cutting infrastructure and platform services — identity, billing, admin, AI foundations.

    Read

docs.title: Connect Core Area — Grounding Context
docs.text: Dialpad Connect is a core Dialpad product. It is the flagship product in the Dialpad suite — the foundation on which Dialpad Support (CCaaS) and Dialpad Sell (sales engagement) are built — and is sold, marketed, and developed as a first-class product line under the Dialpad brand.

docs.title: 1. What Connect Is
docs.text:
Dialpad Connect is Dialpad's AI-powered UCaaS (Unified Communications as a Service) product. It encompasses the core cloud communications experience across voice, video, and messaging — primarily surfaced in the Dialpad and Dialpad Meetings applications.

It owns the user-facing surfaces that let people call, meet, message, and manage their communications environment, while consuming shared capabilities from other core areas (Support, Sell, Ecosystem, Shared Services, Agentic AI).

  • Outcome: Build and maintain the most modern cloud communications platform for businesses.
  • Primary responsibility: End-to-end quality and usability of calling, meetings, messaging, hardware, and admin experiences in the core apps.

docs.title: 2. Scope & Sub-areas
docs.text: Connect's scope is grouped into five sub-areas that frequently collaborate but have distinct responsibilities.

docs.title: 2.1 Daart (Agentic AI in the Connect Surface)
docs.text:
Within Connect, Daart refers to agentic, real-time AI embedded into core communications flows — not the standalone Agentic AI platform itself.

Scope: - In-call and in-meeting agentic experiences (e.g., AI that can answer, ask, and act during a live conversation) - Agentic behaviors inside messaging threads, where appropriate, using the same underlying Agentic services exposed elsewhere - Core app integration: how agentic capabilities show up in the Dialpad UI (entry points, controls, guardrails) — not how the Agentic platform is built or trained

Connect consumes the Agentic AI platform APIs and infrastructure. It does not own the underlying ML models, orchestration, or optimization loops.

docs.title: 2.2 Meetings
docs.text:
Meetings covers the Dialpad Meetings experience and meeting-related capabilities inside the core app.

Scope: - Scheduling and join flows for meetings, including integration into the main Dialpad app experience - In-meeting experience: audio/video, screen sharing, chat, layouts, and collaborative controls - AI in meetings (transcription, summaries, real-time assistance) where surfaced as part of the meetings product UI - Settings and policies for meetings consistent with the broader admin model (RBAC, compliance, device behavior)

Principles: - Reliability and performance are explicit priorities — meeting quality should feel stable and predictable across network conditions and client platforms - Unified, not standalone — meetings differentiate by being part of a single, integrated communications platform, not by chasing feature parity with standalone video products

docs.title: 2.3 Messaging
docs.text:
Messaging covers business messaging across channels directly owned by the core apps (SMS/MMS, team messaging, and related controls) and how these interact with digital channels managed elsewhere.
